headline: GOLD RETREATS TO $4,750 As US Announces Strait of Hormuz Blockade — Iran Talks Collapse After 21 Hours
snippet: Gold fell to below $4,750 as U.S.-Iran peace talks collapsed after 21 hours in Islamabad and Washington announced a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. WTI crude spiked above $100/bbl. Emerging-market central banks selling bullion to defend currencies is adding selling pressure.

headline: MONSTER GRADES: West Red Lake Gold Drills 215 g/t Au Over 5.35m at Madsen — 904 Complex Producing Exceptional Results
snippet: West Red Lake Gold (TSXV: WRLG) released April 13 assays from Madsen Mine's Austin 904 Complex: 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m (incl. 388.90 g/t over 2.95m). A separate hole hit 219.73 g/t Au over 4.8m, including 1,016 g/t over 1m. Initial 904 production is targeted for H1 2027.

headline: Ghana's SLIDING-SCALE GOLD ROYALTY Now Law — Up to 12% Rate at High Prices Threatens Miner Margins
snippet: Ghana enacted a sliding-scale gold royalty on March 9 replacing the flat 5% rate with a 5–12% scale tied to gold price — triggering the maximum band with gold above $4,500. Industry groups warn the change makes Ghana less competitive vs. peers and could deter new investment in Africa's top gold producer.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 171m @ 0.83 g/t Au at Diablillos Argentina — 15,000m Phase VI Underway, DFS Due Q2 2026
snippet: AbraSilver (TSX: ABRA) commenced a 15,000m Phase VI drill campaign at Diablillos, Argentina and reported Phase V results including 171m at 0.83 g/t Au from 230m depth. The project holds 186Moz Ag and 1.6Moz Au in M&I resources. A Definitive Feasibility Study is on track for Q2 2026.

headline: Platinum SURGES 76% in 2026 — Supply Deficit Grows to 480,000 oz as Mine Output Hits 12-Year Low
snippet: Platinum spot is up 76% year-to-date in 2026 as analysts sharply raised price forecasts following tight mine supply and tariff-driven rotation out of gold. The 2026 platinum supply deficit is forecast at 480,000 oz — the largest in years — as mine output falls 2% to a 12-year low. China reclassified platinum as a strategic critical mineral.

headline: Indonesia SLASHES Nickel Ore Quota 34% — Supply Cut Sets Stage for Price Recovery From Multi-Year Lows
snippet: Indonesia cut its 2026 nickel ore production quota to 250–260 million wet metric tonnes, down sharply from 379Mwmt in 2025 — a 34% reduction. The world's largest nickel producer is tightening quotas as prices languish near multi-year lows around $17,000–$18,800/tonne, with EV battery chemistry shifts weighing on demand.

headline: Analysts: Gold Mining Stocks Set to OUTPERFORM the Metal — Record Margins With All-In Costs Below $2,000
snippet: With gold near $4,750/oz and industry all-in sustaining costs below $2,000, producers are generating record free cash flow margins. VanEck and multiple analysts now argue gold equities are positioned to outperform the metal in 2026 — a thesis last validated during the early-2000s bull cycle. Junior miners rose 468% in 2025.

headline: Bolivia's LITHIUM GAMBLE — US Realignment in Latin America Tests Critical Minerals Strategy
snippet: Bolivia, home to the world's largest lithium reserves, is testing a new US engagement strategy as Washington seeks to reshape critical minerals supply chains away from China. The country's state-led lithium model and slow project development have historically deterred Western investment, but geopolitical shifts are forcing a reassessment.

headline: Gold Royalty Corp Hits RECORD Revenue — Q3 Up 37% YoY, Borborema Royalty Acquisition Completed
snippet: Gold Royalty Corp (NYSE: GROY) reported Q3 record revenue of $5.21M — up 37% year-over-year — and completed the acquisition of a royalty on Aura Minerals' Borborema gold project in Brazil. The company targets a 62% increase in gold-equivalent ounces delivered in 2026 as its royalty portfolio matures.

headline: CAMECO CEO: Uncovered Utility Uranium Demand Has Never Been Higher — Forward Demand 'About to Hit the Market'
snippet: Cameco president Grant Isaac says utility uranium requirements not yet contracted are at all-time record highs — 'the forward demand that has yet to come to the market has never been bigger.' Uranium spot surged to $101.41/lb in January 2026. Sprott analysts see long-term price ceilings of $140–$150/lb.
